evals.prompt: I need to create a simple backend API for a new project called 'todo-api'. Just basic auth and user management, nothing fancy.
evals.expected_output: Agent should use brickend init todo-api --template starter, then verify with brickend status. Should NOT add extra bricks unless asked.

assertions.name: uses_starter_template
assertions.text: Agent uses the starter template (not business or multi-tenant)

assertions.name: runs_brickend_init
assertions.text: Agent runs brickend init with the project name 'todo-api'

assertions.name: verifies_with_status
assertions.text: Agent runs brickend status to verify the project was created

evals.prompt: Create a multi-tenant SaaS backend called 'team-hub' with entity management and a product catalog. Each team should have their own workspace.
evals.expected_output: Agent should use brickend init team-hub --template multi-tenant, then add entities and catalog bricks. Should recognize multi-tenant need from 'each team should have their own workspace'.

assertions.name: uses_multi_tenant_template
assertions.text: Agent selects the multi-tenant template based on workspace requirement

assertions.name: adds_catalog_brick
assertions.text: Agent adds the catalog brick for product catalog

assertions.name: correct_order
assertions.text: Agent runs init before add, and adds entities before catalog (dependency order)

evals.prompt: I already have a Brickend project. Can you add entity management to it and show me what's installed?
evals.expected_output: Agent should cd into the project, run brickend add entities, then brickend status. Should NOT run brickend init.

assertions.name: no_init
assertions.text: Agent does NOT run brickend init (project already exists)

assertions.name: shows_status
assertions.text: Agent runs brickend status to show what's installed
